posts.author: aidanweller07
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Chimaev vs. Strickland
Khamzat Chimaev, TKO, R2
I think Khamzat controls him all of round 1 after succeeding on an early shot. After that, I think he’ll realize he can open up more and go for a finish in round 2 and I think he’ll get Sean down again and finish the fight with ground and pound. I’d love to be proven wrong and I do see a path to victory for Sean, but Chimaev is a different animal.

Van vs. Taira
Tatsuro Taira, SUB, R1
I love Joshua Van, but I think this is going to be a very tough night for him. I think the control of Taira is going to be very difficult for him to deal with, and stuffing takedowns off Rei Tsuruya just doesn’t prove much to me. We haven’t seen Van tested much in difficult positions on the ground, and I just feel like Taira is going to get to the back and find the choke late round 1.

Volkov vs. Cortes-Acosta
Alexander Volkov, DEC
I think Volkov will stymie a lot of Waldo’s offense in this fight and utilize the jab and teep to keep him on the outside, and Waldo won’t be able to get inside despite his superior speed. I’m not very confident in this one, but I’m going with Volkov by boring 29-28 decision.

Brady vs. Buckley
Sean Brady, SUB, R2
Very hesitant prediction here, but for some reason I don’t trust that Joaquin is taking grappling training seriously. I know mma math doesn’t work, but here’s a bit of it. Brady controlled Leon Edwards far better and shot with a far higher degree of accuracy than Kamaru Usman did. Usman, once he got Buckley down, was able to do the same. I see little reason to believe Brady can’t get him down, and when he does, he’s an elite jiu jitsu practitioner. I think fans will be very disappointed in Buckley after this fight, myself included.

Green vs. Stephens
King Green, DEC
The hardest hitting 145er is a difficult man to put away. Bobby has looked rejuvenated as of late, and I think he wins a decision in typical fashion, emoting at range and piecing up Stephens to win a 30-27 decision.

Gautier vs. Diaz
Ateba Gautier, TKO, R1
Can we give Gautier a competent opponent please? I like the fighter but I’m not even interested in watching this fight because of how much of an obvious squash match it is for a guy who’s already done that 4 times.

Alvarez vs. Amosov
Yaroslav Amosov, DEC
I believe Amosov will be able to get Alvarez down here, as Joel’s power hasn’t seemed to translate as well to 170 as it did to 155. Yaroslav has enough basic striking to set up his takedowns, and I think Joel will be too willing to accept bottom position and attempt submissions off his back, so I think Amosov wins a control based decision in this spot.

Dawson vs. Rębecki
Mateusz Rębecki, TKO, R1
This is a perfect stylistic matchup for Rebecki. Nobody takes this guy down and controls him, not even the physical beast that is Myktybek Orolbai. Dawson is extremely one dimensional and has poor striking accompanied by a glass chin. Rebecki keeps this on the feet and puts him away pretty early here, really tough matchup for Dawson here.

Miller vs. Gordon
Jared Gordon, DEC
Miller only beats competition of a much lower echelon than what Jared Gordon is these days. I see Gordon out striking him for 15 minutes here and winning a pretty comfortable decision.

Kopylov vs. Tulio
Marco Tulio, DEC
Went back and forth on this one since I feel like Kopylov is the more technical kickboxer in a fight that should mostly take place on the feet and he does have an iron chin. But, I just can’t bring myself to pick a guy who continually throws fights away by being so low output and not throwing. I won’t be surprised at all if I’m proven wrong, but I’d rather bank on Tulio throwing more volume and actually taking initiative to win the fight, although I believe it’ll be a pretty close decision.

Sabatini vs. Gomis
Pat Sabatini, DEC
I am very much not looking forward to this fight, bong is coming out to help survive this one. I think this may go similarly to the Mariscal fight, where Gomis also doesn’t have the power to deter Sabatini’s entries. Sabatini is suffocating on the pressure. The one holdup I do have here is the reach advantage for Gomis, but I’m reasonably confident in Pat’s ability to get to his hips and control him for the better part of 15 minutes

Susurkaev vs. Santos
Baisangur Susurkaev, DEC
I feel like this will either be a really emphatic win or a super close fight. I don’t love Baisangur’s tendency to showboat, but he’s clearly a better all around fighter here and should be able to land more damaging shots on the feet and get control time if he has to. Susurkaev by underwhelming 30-27 decision.

Carpenter vs. Ochoa
Jose Ochoa, TKO, R2
This is admittedly a biased pick since I’m a huge fan of Ochoa, but I do feel confident he gets this one done. Carpenter is tough and a good grappler, but I don’t think he’ll be able to hold Jose down the same way Almabayev did(and that fight was on short notice so Jose has had more time to prepare for a grappler here), and Ochoa is crisp and powerful on the feet. I think Carpenter will end up resorting to panicked takedowns when he feels the power of Ochoa, and that’ll mark the beginning of the end. Ochoa by TKO late round 2.
